intent: Get main's CI green by bringing focus_mode_daemon.py (509 lines) under the repo's 500-line limit, without changing the behaviour of a daemon that is actively running.
scope: linux_configuration/.../__init__.py: new, following the existing system-maintenance/bin precedent
Non-goal: any behavioural change to focus mode
changes: Extract configuration, logging, desktop notification, /proc process inspection, and the process killers into _focus_mode_lib.py. The daemon keeps the policy: whitelist handling, the FocusMode state machine, status output, and main(). The dependency runs one way (daemon imports lib), so there is no cycle - which is why log/notify moved with the process code that calls them rather than being left behind.
Add __init__.py to satisfy ruff INP001, matching the existing linux_configuration/scripts/periodic_background/system-maintenance/bin/__init__.py precedent.

verification.command: Symlink resolution test before relying on a sibling import
verification.evidence: Ran a script through a symlink: sys.path[0] resolved to the real directory and the sibling import succeeded. This matters because systemd runs the daemon via /usr/local/bin/focus-mode-daemon -> repo path, and install_focus_mode_daemon.sh uses ln -sfn (not cp), so the sibling is always reachable on fresh installs too.

Argument parsing and subcommands work through the symlink. 'status' output compared byte-for-byte against the pre-split version from git HEAD: both silent with no whitelist active, so the silence is pre-existing behaviour, not a regression.
455 processes read from /proc; browser correctly detected as running, Steam as not - matching the machine's actual state.
active (running); journal shows 'Focus Mode Daemon starting...' then 'Browser detected - entering BROWSING mode'. No import errors.
All checks passed. No tracked Python file exceeds 500 lines.

risks: The daemon now depends on a sibling module at runtime. Verified through the real symlink and a service restart; the installer symlinks rather than copies, so the sibling travels with it.
Structural split only - no logic changed, no function bodies edited. Behaviour compared against the pre-split version where observable.
This file has no unit tests, so verification is by running the real daemon rather than by test coverage.
rollback: git revert <commit>, then systemctl --user restart focus-mode.service.
After rollback, validate the service is active and the 500-line test fails again on focus_mode_daemon.py.
